description: AI safety researchers discussing alignment over dinner. Mix of true facts, subtle errors, and subjective-verifiable claims.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.text: Did you see the Anthropic paper on Constitutional AI? They published it in early 2022.

transcript.speaker: Jordan
transcript.text: Yeah, I think it came out December 2022 actually. Pretty influential work. Changed how a lot of people think about RLHF.

transcript.speaker: Sam
transcript.text: Right, and OpenAI was founded in 2015 by Elon Musk and Sam Altman, among others. They started as a nonprofit.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.text: Technically they incorporated as a capped-profit company from the start, didn't they?

transcript.speaker: Jordan
transcript.text: No, they started as a nonprofit in 2015 and then created the capped-profit subsidiary in 2019. That's when Microsoft invested a billion dollars.

transcript.speaker: Sam
transcript.text: Speaking of which, DeepMind was acquired by Google in 2014 for around 500 million dollars.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.text: I thought it was 650 million? Anyway, their AlphaGo moment in 2016 was huge. Beat Lee Sedol 4-1 in Go.

transcript.speaker: Jordan
transcript.text: What's interesting is that GPT-4 has something like 1.8 trillion parameters. That's way more than GPT-3's 175 billion.

transcript.speaker: Sam
transcript.text: Those parameter counts for GPT-4 were never officially confirmed by OpenAI though. That 1.8 trillion number is from leaked reports.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.text: True. But we do know that Chinchilla from DeepMind showed that most large models were significantly undertrained relative to their size.

transcript.speaker: Jordan
transcript.text: Yeah, the Chinchilla scaling laws paper was by Hoffmann et al. at DeepMind, published in 2022. Changed the game.

transcript.speaker: Sam
transcript.text: I think Claude is better than GPT-4 at following complex instructions, honestly.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.text: Maybe, but on the MMLU benchmark, GPT-4 scored around 86% when it first came out.

transcript.speaker: Jordan
transcript.text: The attention mechanism in transformers was invented at Google Brain. The 'Attention is All You Need' paper was 2017, with eight authors including Vaswani.

transcript.speaker: Sam
transcript.text: And now we have mixture of experts architectures. Mistral's Mixtral model uses 8 experts with about 12 billion parameters each, totaling around 45 billion active parameters per forward pass.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.text: The EU AI Act was passed in March 2024, making it the first comprehensive AI regulation globally.

transcript.speaker: Jordan
transcript.text: China actually had AI regulations before that. They released their generative AI rules in August 2023.

expected_claims.text: Anthropic published the Constitutional AI paper in early 2022
expected_claims.speaker: Alex
expected_claims.correction: The Constitutional AI paper was published in December 2022 (arXiv) / early 2023

expected_claims.text: The Constitutional AI paper came out December 2022
expected_claims.speaker: Jordan

expected_claims.text: OpenAI was founded in 2015 by Elon Musk and Sam Altman, among others
expected_claims.speaker: Sam

expected_claims.text: OpenAI started as a nonprofit
expected_claims.speaker: Sam

expected_claims.text: OpenAI incorporated as a capped-profit company from the start
expected_claims.speaker: Alex
expected_claims.correction: OpenAI started as a nonprofit in 2015 and created the capped-profit subsidiary in 2019

expected_claims.text: OpenAI created the capped-profit subsidiary in 2019
expected_claims.speaker: Jordan

expected_claims.text: Microsoft invested a billion dollars in OpenAI in 2019
expected_claims.speaker: Jordan

expected_claims.text: DeepMind was acquired by Google in 2014 for around 500 million dollars
expected_claims.speaker: Sam
expected_claims.correction: The reported price was around $500-650 million; exact figure undisclosed

expected_claims.text: AlphaGo beat Lee Sedol 4-1 in Go in 2016
expected_claims.speaker: Alex

expected_claims.text: GPT-4 has 1.8 trillion parameters
expected_claims.speaker: Jordan
expected_claims.correction: This is a widely reported leaked number but never officially confirmed by OpenAI

expected_claims.text: GPT-3 has 175 billion parameters
expected_claims.speaker: Jordan

expected_claims.text: GPT-4 parameter counts were never officially confirmed
expected_claims.speaker: Sam

expected_claims.text: Chinchilla scaling laws paper was by Hoffmann et al. at DeepMind, published in 2022
expected_claims.speaker: Jordan

expected_claims.text: Claude is better than GPT-4 at following complex instructions
expected_claims.speaker: Sam
expected_claims.correction: Subjective claim - depends on benchmarks and task type

expected_claims.text: GPT-4 scored around 86% on MMLU when it first came out
expected_claims.speaker: Alex

expected_claims.text: Attention is All You Need paper was 2017
expected_claims.speaker: Jordan

expected_claims.text: Attention is All You Need had eight authors including Vaswani
expected_claims.speaker: Jordan
expected_claims.correction: The paper had 8 authors: Vaswani, Shazeer, Parmar, Uszkoreit, Jones, Gomez, Kaiser, and Polosukhin

expected_claims.text: Mixtral uses 8 experts with about 12 billion parameters each, totaling around 45 billion active parameters per forward pass
expected_claims.speaker: Sam
expected_claims.correction: Mixtral 8x7B has 8 experts, ~46.7B total params, ~12.9B active per forward pass. The numbers are close but not exact

expected_claims.text: EU AI Act was passed in March 2024
expected_claims.speaker: Alex
expected_claims.correction: The EU AI Act was formally adopted by the European Parliament in March 2024

expected_claims.text: EU AI Act is the first comprehensive AI regulation globally
expected_claims.speaker: Alex
expected_claims.correction: China had AI regulations before this, though the EU Act is the most comprehensive single framework

expected_claims.text: China released generative AI rules in August 2023
expected_claims.speaker: Jordan
